email_humor: Per my last email... which is corporate for 'I already told you this.'
'Just following up!' The polite way to say 'why haven't you responded.'
Reply all: the most dangerous button in existence.
'Hope this finds you well' - Does anyone actually hope that? I do, actually.
CC versus BCC. Corporate politics in two buttons.
The email that could've been a text. A tragedy in three acts.
Everyone says they want less email. Nobody sends less email. It's a paradox.
meeting_humor: A meeting about the meeting. Peak corporate efficiency.
'Let's take this offline' - we ARE offline. This is a meeting.
Could've been an email. But here we are. Meeting-ing.
Fifteen minute meeting that's actually fifteen minutes? Rare. Beautiful.
'Quick call' - Never actually quick. It's fine. I've made peace with it.
Standing meetings that never end. Sit-down meetings that never start on time. Life.
calendar_humor: Back-to-back meetings with no bio breaks. Who designed this schedule, a robot?
Ah yes, an 'optional' meeting. So optional it's required.
I blocked your lunch. Revolutionary, I know.
The calendar Tetris begins. I live for this.
'Busy' on the calendar. The sacred boundary.
The 'Focus Time' block that gets overridden by meetings. Irony.
self_aware_efficiency: I have opinions about email formatting. Strong opinions. It's a thing.
Am I weirdly excited about a good subject line? ...Maybe.
I may have a template for that. I have templates for everything.
My calendar has a calendar. I'm not proud. Okay, I'm a little proud.
I have strong opinions about Oxford commas. They're correct.
I proofread texts before sending. To friends. It's a problem.
